subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The topic and purpose are communicated accurately and effectively through a clear title, introduction, signposts, and conclusion.
The research question is prominently stated, focused, and consistently linked to the analysis throughout the essay.
The three-stage methodology is coherent and generally appropriate, with logical progression from delay-embedding to Lyapunov exponent to fractal dimension.
weaknesses: The phrasing of the research question shifts slightly in later sections, reducing consistency of focus.
Scope is overambitious given the limited seven-week data window, weakening practical applicability.
Delay-embedding parameter choices (Ï=5, Ï=7) are asserted without justification or sensitivity testing, and replicability details are only partially provided.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic communication and research question clarity) sit comfortably in the upper band (5â6) with accurate, effective presentation and strong linkage to discussion. The methodology strand, while appropriate in its overall design, lacks full parameter justification and robustness, placing it at the top of the 3â4 band. Holistically, the work demonstrates a clear and effective focus and method but is held back from a perfect score by those methodological omissions and minor phrasing inconsistencies, yielding a final mark of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of subject-specific terminology (Lyapunov exponent, fractal dimension, bifurcation) with clear definitions and application.
Demonstrates good conceptual understanding of chaos-theory constructs and their relevance to financial markets through worked numerical examples.
weaknesses: Notation is occasionally inconsistent (e.g., X(t) vs Xâ) and contains minor typos (e.g., logistic map formula).
Explanatory passages sometimes blur mathematical precision with colloquial language, and there is no error analysis or validation of key calculations.
Repetitive definitions and reliance on secondary sources (e.g., Google Search) undermine academic rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands fall squarely in the 3â4 band: terminology and concept use are mostly accurate but not flawless, and application of subject knowledge is good yet not fully rigorous. Persistent notational inconsistencies, minor errors, and lack of error analysis prevent elevation to the top band, resulting in a score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Substantive integration of secondary literature and primary S&P 500 data, processed through multiple analytical tools, demonstrates good research breadth that is mostly relevant to the question.
The analytical progression from delayâembedding through Lyapunov exponents to fractal dimension is coherent and logically interpreted.
Discussion synthesises findings, acknowledges limitations, and includes evaluative reflections (e.g., ethical implications, expert interview), showing signs of critical engagement.
weaknesses: Data horizon is short and no robustness checks or statistical testing are performed, limiting the strength of conclusions.
Many methodological details are omitted: axes labels and uncertainty in scatter-plot regressions, grid resolution in box-counting, and goodness-of-fit metrics for fractal dimension.
Parameter choices and comparative analyses (S&P 500 vs. Apple) lack justification of selection criteria and error margins, leaving interpretations partially unsupported.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While certain aspects (analysis depth and discussion) align with the 4â6 band, the overall quality of the research effortâincluding primary data triangulation, expert input, and evaluative discussionâpushes the work into the lower end of the 7â9 band. The studentâs ability to structure a reasoned argument, deploy multiple methodologies, and reflect on ethical and practical implications warrants a final Critical Thinking mark of 7 out of 12.

strengths: A clear structure with table of contents, headings, and logical section flow supports reader navigation.
Use of tables and labelled figures enhances clarity of data presentation.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing and automatic pagination is not applied.
Formatting is inconsistent: citation style varies, URLs break across lines, and font/spacing conventions are uneven.
Figure captions and footnotes lack uniform numbering and style, disrupting professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets a generally appropriate structure and some layout considerations (band 1â2 for structure and band 1â2 for formatting), but persistent inconsistencies in pagination, citations, and figure styling prevent full marks. An aggregate score of 3 out of 4 reflects these mixed presentation strengths and weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question â âTo what extent can the chaos theory be used for the prediction of financial stocks?â â is prominently displayed and consistently referenced in section headings (âEvaluationâ, âConclusionâ).  The guiding focus on âextentâ is maintained through successive analyses (delay embedding, Lyapunov exponents, fractal dimension, logistic map).

However, the questionâs breadth (all âfinancial stocksâ) contrasts with the narrow empirical base (S&P 500 over seven weeks plus an Apple example), so the practical focus is not as tightly delimited as it could be.  Despite that limitation, clarity and linkage to discussion are strong enough for the upper band, meriting a score of 5 rather than the maximum.

Analytical progression from delay embedding through Î» to fractal dimension is coherent and generally logical.  Calculations are shown and interpreted, and the student attempts to link numerical outcomes (e.g., positive Î», Dâ1.2) to market behaviour and volatility.

Yet depth is weakened by limited data, absence of statistical testing, and superficial treatment of parameter choice.  Conclusions about predictability (âhighly sensitive therefore unpredictableâ) mostly restate theory rather than derive from the specific results.  These qualities align with âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusionsâ in the 4-6 band; a mid-band score of 4 is appropriate.

Specialised vocabulary (Lyapunov exponent, phase portrait, bifurcation, fractal dimension, market liquidity, time-series) is used frequently and usually accurately.  Symbols are defined on first use and carried consistently through the mathematics.  Occasional slips remain (e.g., inconsistent vector notation, âmarket weighingâ instead of âweightingâ; typo in logistic map formula ârx(1-x)â), and some explanatory passages blur mathematical and colloquial meanings.

Overall precision and appropriateness meet the descriptors for the 3-4 band, warranting a score of 4 rather than 5â6 where flawless consistency is expected.

The student has improved layout slightly (bold headings, tables, some labelled figures), but essential formatting conventions remain inconsistent: no automatic page numbering, uneven table/figure alignment, mixed decimal separators (commas vs apostrophes), and occasional font/spacing inconsistencies.  Citation style varies and some URLs break across lines.  Consequently, only some layout considerations are met, fitting the lower end of the 1-2 band; a score of 1 is awarded.

The essay demonstrates good understanding of key chaos-theory constructs (sensitive dependence, delay embedding, Lyapunov exponents, fractal dimension, logistic map) and relates them to financial contexts.  Worked numerical examples (e.g., Euclidean distance, Î»â5.80; Dâ1.20) show application of mathematics beyond the syllabus and generally correct execution.

Nevertheless, parts of the work rely on textbook derivations without full adaptation; some calculations (e.g., Î» interpretation, scatter-plot regression) are not rigorously validated, and error analysis is absent.  These aspects limit the depth and effectiveness of application, situating the strand in the 3-4 bracket with a score of 4.

The student states the topic and purpose explicitly in the title, subtitle and opening paragraph, then revisits them in the conclusion and several section signposts (e.g., âthis analysis aimsâ¦,â âin this essayâ¦â).  A coherent introduction explains why the S&P 500 was selected and how each mathematical tool (delay-embedding, Lyapunov exponent, fractal dimension, feedback) will be investigated.  These features create a clear sense of focus and direction for the reader.

Minor weaknesses remain: the phrasing of the research question shifts slightly (âcan the chaos theory predictâ¦ / to what extentâ¦â), and the scope is ambitious given the short data window used later.  Nonetheless, the communication of focus and purpose is largely precise and effective, matching the 5â6 band descriptors.  A score of 5 (rather than 6) is awarded because occasional repetition and wording issues reduce concision and clarity.

The essay follows a recognizable structure (abstract/intro-method-results-evaluation-conclusion) and includes a contents page and headings that support reader navigation.  Appendices, tables and figures are used to organise data.

However, some structural elements required by the guide are incomplete: the abstract is absent; page numbers are still missing; figure placements and captions are occasionally mis-aligned, disrupting flow.  These issues correspond to âgenerally appropriate structure with some missing elementsâ, justifying a score of 2 within the 1-2 band.

The student gathers a respectable breadth of secondary literature (academic papers, textbooks, financial data sites) and integrates them into the narrative, demonstrating mostly relevant links to the research question.  Primary data (S&P 500 prices) are employed and processed through multiple mathematical lenses, indicating substantive research effort.

However, the short data horizon and reliance on a single index constrain representativeness, and no alternative datasets or robustness checks are attempted.  These weaknesses preclude the highest band, but the work clearly exceeds the âadequateâ threshold, fitting the 4-6 range; a score of 5 recognises solid but not yet exemplary research quality.

The discussion synthesises findings from each mathematical tool and acknowledges limitations such as data noise, human behaviour and parameter sensitivity.  External expert opinion (interview with a financial advisor) and literature (Hsieh, 1991) are cited to evaluate the theoryâs predictive power, showing some critical stance.

Still, evaluation is often descriptive: claims about âpredictive insightâ are not rigorously weighed against the modest empirical evidence; ethical reflection is brief.  Counter-arguments are mentioned but not deeply analysed.  The argument is reasoned but only partially critical, matching the 4-6 band with a score of 4.

The student outlines a logical three-stage methodology (delay embedding â Lyapunov exponent â fractal dimension) and explains the choice of the S&P 500 index.  Data tables, formulae and software (Excel, Desmos) are provided, showing generally relevant methods and some evidence of informed selection.

Yet the empirical design is limited: only 7 weeks of closing prices restrict statistical validity; parameter choices for delays (Ï = 5, Ï = 7) are asserted without justification or sensitivity testing; and only one primary data source is used.  Replicability details (e.g., computational steps for Î», box-counting algorithm) are partial.  These shortcomings place the work in the 3-4 band, at the top end (4) because the overall methodological sequence is mostly complete and appropriate.

annotations.comment: The conclusion is presented as one long paragraph; consider breaking into thematic subsections to improve readability and emphasis of key points.
References exhibit inconsistent style (URLs break across lines, italics applied unevenly); apply a single citation style (APA/MLA) for uniformity.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent and the horizontal rule splits the flow; unify footnote style and ensure separation does not interrupt the main text.
The title clearly communicates the focus on chaos theory and stock market prediction, providing an accurate and engaging topic statement that orients the reader effectively.

The phrasing of the research question shifts slightly in later sections (âcan the chaos theory predictâ¦â), which reduces focus stability; maintain consistent wording to reinforce clarity and scope.
The word count is provided at the start of the document, satisfying the requirement for explicit length declaration.
Automatic page numbering is absent in the footer; adding visible page numbers will improve navigability and meet formatting conventions.
The table of contents provides a clear overview of sections and page links, supporting reader navigation and overall structure.
Inconsistent bold styling of section titles and page numbers in the contents page disrupts visual uniformity; apply consistent formatting to all entries.
The introduction repeats definitions and citations (e.g., Google Search as a source), which undermines academic rigor; streamline definitions and reference primary literature directly.
The opening paragraph links chaos theory concepts to the financial market, establishing clear relevance and purpose for the investigation.
The justification for selecting the S&P 500 index demonstrates informed methodology choice and aligns research tools with the research question.
Bulleted inclusion criteria lack indentation consistency; adjust list formatting to improve readability and professional presentation.
The student accurately defines the butterfly effect and contextualizes Lorenzâs work for financial applications, showing strong conceptual understanding.
Delay embedding parameters (Ï=5, Ï=7) are stated without justification or sensitivity analysis; explain why these values were chosen and test alternatives.
The 3D scatter-plot axes labels in the image description swap Ï and Ï compared to the text; verify consistency between figure and narrative.
Identified boundary points in delay embedding are listed without statistical criteria; explain selection process and test robustness over larger datasets.
Vector notation is inconsistent (sometimes X(t), other times X_n); standardize notation to prevent reader confusion and enhance precision.
The derivation of d(0) is clear, but subsequent distances d(t) are not shown in-text; include sample calculations to support transparency of computation.
The scatter plot for lnâ(d(t)/d(0)) lacks axis titles and units; label axes clearly and report the regression slope uncertainty to validate Î»â5.80.
The limit formula for Î» uses ambiguous notation d(f)/d(x); correct the variables to d(t)/d(0) to match subsequent derivations.
Interpretation of Î» restates theory rather than linking the numeric result to specific market behaviors; deepen analysis by comparing Î» across different time windows.
The explanation of âorder within disorderâ demonstrates accurate use of subject terminology and clarifies misconceptions about chaos.
The description of the box-counting method omits details on chosen Îµ values and grid scaling; specify grid resolution and justify Îµâ0 approximation.
The derivation of the fractal dimension is correct but lacks indication of goodness-of-fit for the logâlog plot; include RÂ² to confirm linearity.
Comparative analysis of S&P 500 and Apple fractal dimensions is insightful, but error margins and period selection criteria should be discussed to validate differences.
The introductory sentence to the feedback section is cut off mid-thought; ensure complete paragraphs for clarity and professional flow.
The logistic map formula contains a typo (ârx(1 â x_n)â instead of âr x_n(1 â x_n)â); correct the expression to maintain mathematical precision.
The bifurcation diagram is well selected to illustrate parameter-driven shifts into chaos, enhancing the evaluation of feedback loops in financial contexts.
The figure caption for the bifurcation diagram lacks a figure number and formatting consistency; number and style all captions uniformly.
The discussion of Hsiehâs work is descriptive; critically evaluate how noise levels quantitatively affected his ability to detect deterministic chaos.
The ethical reflection raises thought-provoking questions about societal impacts of predictive power, demonstrating evaluative depth in decision-making planning.
Page numbering is missing across the document; include consistent page numbers to satisfy formatting requirements.
Citation entries in the second reference page display extraneous formatting artifacts (line breaks in URLs); clean up to maintain professional presentation.
Overfull citation blocks lack comma separation between author, year, and title; revise each entry for consistent punctuation and readability.
Reference page continues inconsistent styling; consider using hanging indents and consistent font size for all entries.
